\[
\mathcal {C} = \sum_ {i j \in E ^ {-}} w _ {i j} ^ {-} \mathbb {1} _ {[ i, j \text {i n d i f f e r e n t c l u s t e r s} ]} + \sum_ {i j \in E ^ {+}} w _ {i j} ^ {+} \mathbb {1} _ {[ i, j \text {i n t h e s a m e c l u s t e r} ]}.

Input: Input data for (BoundedSDP), stopping criteria \(\epsilon\), accuracy parameter \(\eta\), upper bound \(C_g^u\) on the curvature constant, probability \(p\) for the subproblem LMO  
Output: \(z \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \widehat{X}_{\epsilon})\) and \(v = \mathcal{B}(\widehat{X}_{\epsilon})\), where \(\widehat{X}_{\epsilon}\) is an \(\epsilon\)-optimal solution of (BoundedSDP)  
1 Function FWGaussian:  
2 Select initial point \(X_0 \in S\); set \(v_0 \gets \mathcal{B}(X_0)\) and sample \(z_0 \sim \mathcal{N}(0, X_0)\)  
3 \(t \gets 0\), \(\gamma \gets 2/(t+2)\)  
4 \((h_t, q_t) \gets \mathsf{LMO}(\mathcal{B}^*(\nabla g(v_t)), \frac{1}{2}\eta\gamma C_g^u)\)  
5 while \(\langle q_t - v_t, \nabla g(v_t) \rangle > \epsilon\) do  
6 \((z_{t+1}, v_{t+1}) \gets \mathsf{UpdateVariable}(z_t, v_t, h_t, q_t, \gamma)\)  
7 \(t \gets t+1\), \(\gamma \gets 2/(t+2)\)  
8 \((h_t, q_t) \gets \mathsf{LMO}(\mathcal{B}^*(\nabla g(v_t)), \frac{1}{2}\eta\gamma C_g^u, p)\)  
9 end  
10 return \((z_t, v_t)\)  
11 Function LMO(J, δ):  
12 Find a unit vector \(h\) such that with probability at least \(1 - p\),  
13 if \(\lambda \geq 0\) then \(q \gets \mathcal{B}(\alpha hh^T)\)  
14 else \(q \gets 0\), \(h \gets 0\)  
15 return \((h, q)\)  
16 Function UpdateVariable(z, v, h, q, \(\gamma\)):  
17 \(z \gets (\sqrt{1 - \gamma})z + \sqrt{\gamma\alpha} h\zeta\) where \(\zeta \sim \mathcal{N}(0, 1)\)  
18 \(v \gets (1 - \gamma)v + \gamma q\)  
19 return \((z, v)\)

\[

\]

\[
\max  _ {X \succeq 0} \left\{\langle C, X \rangle - \beta \phi_ {M} \left(\operatorname {d i a g} (X) - \mathbb {1}, - \frac {1}{k - 1} - e _ {i} ^ {T} X e _ {j}\right): (i, j) \in E, \operatorname {T r} (X) \leq n \right\}. \quad (\mathrm {k - C u t - L S E})
\]

Input: Sample \(z_{i}\sim \mathcal{N}(0,\widehat{X}_{\epsilon})\) for \(i = 1,\dots ,k\) and \(\mathrm{diag}(\widehat{X}_\epsilon)\) Output: \(z_i^f\sim \mathcal{N}(0,X^f)\) for \(i = 1,\ldots ,k\) with \(X^{f}\) feasible to (k-Cut-Rel)   
1 Function GeneratekSamples:   
2 for \(i = 1,\ldots ,k\) do   
3 Set err \(= \max \{0,\max_{(i,j)\in E,i <   j} - 1 / (k - 1) - [\widehat{X}_\epsilon ]_{ij}\}\)   
4 Set \(\overline{z}_i = z_i + \sqrt{\mathrm{err}} y\mathbb{1},\) where \(y\sim \mathcal{N}(0,1)\)   
5 Generate \(\zeta \sim \mathcal{N}\left(0,I - \mathrm{diag}^*\left(\frac{\mathrm{diag}(\widehat{X}_\epsilon) + \mathrm{err}}{\mathrm{max}(\mathrm{diag}(\widehat{X}_\epsilon)) + \mathrm{err}}\right)\right)\)   
6 Set \(z_i^f = \frac{\overline{z}_i}{\sqrt{\max(\mathrm{diag}(\widehat{X}_\epsilon)) + \mathrm{err}}} +\zeta\)   
7 end   
8 return \(z_1^f,\dots ,z_k^f\)
